response.justification: Bogdan presents a strong alignment with the role's AI-native engineering expectations — his current position at re:cinq involves MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure, which maps directly to the environment described. His high-throughput distributed systems background at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests per second) demonstrates the architectural depth the role requires. However, Python — listed as a must-have — is entirely absent from his resume, which is a significant and unresolved gap. The distributed systems experience is also primarily from 2018–2020, with more recent roles leaning toward product-level TypeScript work. There is no explicit evidence of on-call rotations, incident response, or cross-functional stakeholder management at scale.
strengths: Direct hands-on experience building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP-based agentic tooling in his current role — a strong match for the AI-native engineering environment
Proven high-throughput distributed systems background handling millions of requests per second with low-latency, async I/O design at Dunnhumby
Consistent staff-level technical leadership across multiple companies, complemented by public knowledge-sharing via workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume despite being listed as a must-have skill — no evidence of proficiency at any level
High-throughput and distributed systems experience is largely dated (2018–2020); more recent work at Optilyz and re:cinq is narrower in architectural scope
No explicit mention of operational ownership, on-call rotations, or incident response — key requirements for this role that are unaddressed in the resume
